text: This research assesses the capacity of Large Language Models (LLMs) to represent geometric structures and their respective spatial relations. Utilizing BERT and GPT-2, we encoded geometries provided in Well-Known Text (WKT) format and processed the resulting embeddings through downstream classifiers and regressors to evaluate how well these models capture geometric attributes. Our experiments reveal that while LLM-generated embeddings effectively preserve geometry types and identify certain spatial relations (achieving up to 73% accuracy), they struggle significantly with precise numeric estimations, such as area and centroid calculations, and the retrieval of spatially related objects. The findings suggest that current foundation models lose critical quantitative nuance during tokenization. This research underscores the necessity of integrating domain-specific geospatial knowledge and developing more robust encoding strategies to support complex GeoAI applications.
